preamble: The spirantization of voiceless aspirated dental stops into dental fricatives is a key historical shift. This change is famously observed in the development of Ancient Greek, where θ was originally an aspirated stop [tʰ] and later became the fricative [θ].

examples.note: θ (theta) originally represented [tʰ] but shifted to [θ] by the Koine period.
PIE *t > Proto-Germanic *θ through an intermediate aspirated stage *tʰ.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
